zh.Liskeard, Cornwall Unitary Authority, Co: 英國英格蘭可瓦爾利斯可亞爾德
zh.St. Luke's Churchyard, Hodnet, Shropshi: 英國英格蘭史洛普爾霍德特
zh.Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England: 英國英格蘭史洛普爾史福斯波利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder於1510年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾時，她的父親James Roger Hill27歲，母親Ellen Leche30歲。她於1542年10月2日與William Charles Ryder在英國英格蘭史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1545年4月22日在英國英格蘭享年35歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭史洛普爾霍德特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder于1510年出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾时，她的父亲James Roger Hill27岁，母亲Ellen Leche30岁。她于1542年10月2日与William Charles Ryder在英国英格兰史洛普爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1545年4月22日在英国英格兰享年35岁，埋葬在英国英格兰史洛普爾霍德特。

nameMeanings.name: Hill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, from Middle English hill, hell, hull ‘hill’ (Old English hyll). Compare Hiller . This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: possibly in some cases from the personal name Hille, a pet form of some name such as Hilger or Hillary .
German: from a short form of Hildebrand or any of a variety of other names, male and female, containing ancient Germanic hild ‘battle’ as the first element.

variants.name: Hillock
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) small hill’, from Middle English hillok, a diminutive of hille (Old English hyll ‘hill’). See Hill .

variants.name: Hills
meanings: English: variant of Hill with genitival -s if a relationship name, attested in Matilda Hilles, 1327 (Somerset), where the genitival form may signify widowhood; or with plural -s if habitational, attested in Johanne de Hyles, 1379 (Bickerstaffe, Lancashire), and Ricardo del Hyles, 1379 (Great Eccleston, Lancashire); or, with post-medieval excrescent -s, probably the most common form.

variants.name: Hiller
meanings: German: metronymic from Hille, a pet form of the female personal name Hildegund (see Hilke ).
German: from a masculine personal beginning with the ancient Germanic element hild ‘strife, battle’.
German: variant of Hüller (see Huller ).

variants.name: Hil
meanings: Altered form of English   Hill  .
Dutch (Van den Hil): topographic name from hil, hul ‘hill’, particularly in the sense of a flight hill above sea level in the southwestern Netherlands.

nameMeanings.name: Ryder
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Rider  .
Americanized and Flemish form of Dutch Rijder: occupational name for a mounted warrior or messenger, Middle Dutch rider ‘horseman, rider’.

variants.name: Rider
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English ridere ‘horseman’, perhaps with a specific application such as ‘forest ranger’, ‘horseman who guards crops’, or ‘mounted warrior, knight’. Compare Knight .
English: habitational name from Ryther, near Selby (Yorkshire), probably from Old English ryther ‘clearing’.

English: topographic name from Middle English ridere ‘dweller at a clearing’ (see Reeder ) or ‘dweller by a small stream’, depending on which sense of Middle English ride the surname is derived from. These topographic senses of Rider are limited to the southern counties of England (especially Surrey, Sussex, and Hampshire), where topographic names in -er and the Middle English words ride ‘clearing’ and rithe ‘small stream’ are mainly found. See also Reed .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘Morning dew’; also ‘generosity’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Baber
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Muslim name, from Turkish: ‘lion’, the byname of Zahir ud-Din Muhammad ( c.1482–1530 ), first of the Mogul rulers in India.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder was born in 1510, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, James Roger Hill, was 27 and her mother, Ellen Leche, was 30. She married William Charles Ryder on 2 October 1542, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 22 April 1545, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 35, and was buried in Hodnet, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder est née en 1510, à Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, James Roger Hill, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Ellen Leche, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé William Charles Ryder le 2 octobre 1542, à Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 22 avril 1545, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 35 ans, et a été enterrée à Hodnet, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder 1510 in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater James Roger Hill 27 und ihre Mutter Ellen Leche 30. Sie hat am 2. Oktober 1542 in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom William Charles Ryder geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 22. April 1545 im Alter von 35 Jahren in England, United Kingdom und wurde in Hodnet, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder è nata il nel 1510 nel Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, James Roger Hill, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Ellen Leche, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato William Charles Ryder il 2 ottobre 1542, nel Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 22 aprile 1545, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 35 anni, e fu sepolta a Hodnet, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryderが1510年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアで生まれた時，父James Roger Hillは27歳，母Ellen Lecheは30歳であった。1542年10月2日にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアでWilliam Charles Ryderと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1545年4月22日にイギリスイギリスで35歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアホードネットにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder(이)가 1510년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James Roger Hill(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ellen Leche(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1542년 10월 2일 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 William Charles Ryder(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1545년 4월 22일 영국 잉글랜드에서 35세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 호드넷에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder nasceu em 1510, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, James Roger Hill, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Ellen Leche, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com William Charles Ryder em 2 de outubro de 1542, em Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 22 de abril de 1545, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 35 anos, e foi sepultada em Hodnet, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder родилась в 1510 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени James Roger Hill было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Ellen Leche, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Charles Ryder 2 октября 1542 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 22 апреля 1545 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 35 лет и была похоронена в местности ходнет, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Margaret Baber Hill Ryder nació en 1510, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, James Roger Hill, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Ellen Leche, tenía 30 años. Se casó con William Charles Ryder el 2 de octubre de 1542, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 22 de abril de 1545, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 35 años, y fue sepultada en Hodnet, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
